title: Fluffy Canadian Lamb Poutine
description: A rich, comforting twist on the classic Canadian dish, featuring a flaky, long-fermentation dough crust topped with tender lamb, velvety cheese curds, and a drizzle of sesame seed oil.

ingredients.name: Long-fermentation dough crust
ingredients.notes: Homemade or store-bought, thawed according to package instructions.

ingredients.name: Ground lamb
ingredients.notes: Leaner cuts such as sirloin or round work best.

ingredients.name: Cheese curds
ingredients.notes: Fresh or frozen, thawed according to package instructions.

instructions: Roll out the long-fermentation dough crust to a thickness of about 1/8 inch (3 mm).
Transfer the dough to a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, trimming the edges as needed.
In a large skillet, sauté the onion and garlic over medium heat until the onion is translucent.
Add the ground lamb to the skillet, breaking it up with a spoon as it cooks.
Season the lamb mixture with salt and black pepper to taste.
Transfer the lamb mixture to a small saucepan and stir in the chicken broth.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook until thickened, stirring occasionally.
Spread the cooked lamb mixture over the dough, leaving a 1-inch (2.5 cm) border around the edges.
Top the lamb mixture with cheese curds.
Bake the poutine in the preheated oven for 15-20 minutes, or until the crust is golden brown and the cheese is melted.
Remove the poutine from the oven and drizzle with sesame seed oil before serving.
